content.text: Everything needed is in the toolbox. I'll regenerate node and client certs signed by the existing CA, valid 365 days.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
set -e
cd /tmp/certs
# client cnf (CN=root, clientAuth)
cat > client.root.cnf <<EOF
[req]
distinguished_name = dn
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no
[dn]
CN = root
[v3_req]
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
EOF

# fresh keys
openssl genrsa -out node.new.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl genrsa -out client.root.new.key 2048 2>/dev/null

# CSRs
openssl req -new -key node.new.key -out node.new.csr -config node.cnf
openssl req -new -key client.root.new.key -out client.root.new.csr -config client.root.cnf

# sign 365 days
openssl x509 -req -in node.new.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out node.new.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extfile node.cnf -extensions v3_req 2>/dev/null
openssl x509 -req -in client.root.new.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.root.new.crt -days 365 -sha256 -extfile client.root.cnf -extensions v3_req 2>/dev/null

echo "=== new node cert ==="
openssl x509 -in node.new.crt -noout -enddate -subject
openssl x509 -in node.new.crt -noout -text | grep -A3 "Subject Alternative"
echo "=== new client cert ==="
openssl x509 -in client.root.new.crt -noout -enddate -subject
echo "=== verify against CA ==="
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt node.new.crt
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt client.root.new.crt
'

content.text: New certs valid until Jun 2027 (~1 year), correct SANs. Now I'll update the secret with the new node and client cert/key pairs.

input.command:
cd /tmp
for f in node.new.crt node.new.key client.root.new.crt client.root.new.key; do
  kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/$f > /tmp/$f
done
NODE_CRT=$(base64 -w0 /tmp/node.new.crt)
NODE_KEY=$(base64 -w0 /tmp/node.new.key)
CLI_CRT=$(base64 -w0 /tmp/client.root.new.crt)
CLI_KEY=$(base64 -w0 /tmp/client.root.new.key)
kubectl -n cockroachdb patch secret crdb-cluster-certs --type merge -p "{\"data\":{\"node.crt\":\"$NODE_CRT\",\"node.key\":\"$NODE_KEY\",\"client.root.crt\":\"$CLI_CRT\",\"client.root.key\":\"$CLI_KEY\"}}" 2>&1
